summary: This source outlines Aristotle's historical significance, his vast body of work across multiple disciplines, and his teleological approach to ethics. It highlights his core belief that the best human life is objective, not subjective, and must be lived in accordance with reason.

principles.statement: The best life for a human being is lived in accordance with reason.
principles.rationale: After reflecting on the criteria for a successful life, a life of reason uniquely meets the requirements for the superior form of human life.
principles.source_quote: This is a life lived in accordance with reason.

principles.statement: The best life is not a matter of subjective preference.
principles.rationale: If the good life were merely subjective, people wouldn't make choices that lead to sub-optimal lives. Because there is an objective standard, people can fail to meet it.
principles.source_quote: Because he believes that the best life for a human being is not a matter of subjective preference, he also believes that people can (and, sadly, often do) choose to lead sub-optimal lives.

frameworks.name: Reflection on the Best Life
frameworks.when_to_apply: When trying to avoid leading a sub-optimal or unhappy life.
steps: Acknowledge the desire to lead a good life.
Reflect on the criteria any successful candidate for the best life must satisfy.
Propose and pursue the kind of life that uniquely meets those criteria.
frameworks.source_quote: Aristotle recommends reflection on the criteria any successful candidate for the best life must satisfy.

mental_models.name: Teleological Framework
mental_models.description: A lens that evaluates subjects, including ethics and politics, based on their ultimate purpose or end goal (e.g., the pursuit of the 'best life').
mental_models.example: Using teleology to determine that the ultimate goal of human beings is to live a life in accordance with reason.

anti_patterns.description: Treating the best life as a matter of subjective preference.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It causes people to choose sub-optimal lives because they fail to reflect on the objective criteria required for human flourishing.
